response.justification: While Bogdan is a capable polyglot engineer with strong software craftsmanship fundamentals, his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His primary expertise for the past decade has been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang; C++ appears only as a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project — neither demonstrates the depth, scale, or recency required for a mid-senior C++ architect role. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IoT protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or familiarity with IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle standards.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance, low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby (async I/O, fibers, millions of requests/sec)
Event-driven architecture experience through the Northwind Labs geo-data platform built on Node.js
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active knowledge-sharing through workshops and open-source
concerns: C++ experience is limited to ~6 months (NI, 2015) and a student thesis — well below the 8–12 years of hands-on C++ development the role demands
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, or relevant protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
No evidence of multi-threaded C++ systems design, STL-heavy architectures, or secure development lifecycle implementation in an industrial or embedded context
